Functional Modules.Module Name: USB-C PD Input & Battery Charger with Power-Path Management
Functional Modules.Function Description: Manages USB-C Power Delivery input, charges a single-cell lithium-ion/polymer battery, and dynamically routes power from either source to the main system voltage rail with priority and seamless switching.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: USB-C connector (VBUS, CC1/CC2), Battery terminal (BAT+), System load demand.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Main System Voltage Rail (SYS), Battery (for charging), Automatic Power OR-ing Network (as a source), System microcontroller (charging status, fault indicators).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: An integrated switching battery charger IC with built-in power-path management. It negotiates USB PD contracts, regulates battery charge current/voltage (CC/CV), and uses internal MOSFETs to select the higher-voltage source (USB or battery) to power the SYS rail, ensuring no back-powering and minimal dropout.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides seamless, glitch-free transition between the main SYS rail and a backup capacitor/battery for critical logic, generating a stable 3.3V rail immune to input transients.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Main System Voltage Rail (SYS) from Charger module, Optional backup capacitor bank.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: 3.3V Rail (for core logic, MCU, memory), System monitoring circuits.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A power OR-ing circuit using Schottky diodes or ideal diode controllers to combine SYS and a local reservoir. Its output feeds a low-noise, high-PSRR Low-Dropout (LDO) linear regulator set to 3.3V, ensuring stability during USB plug/unplug events.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Steps up the variable main system voltage (e.g., 3.0V-4.2V from battery) to a stable 5V rail to power peripheral interfaces.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Main System Voltage Rail (SYS), Enable signal from system controller.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: 5V Power Distribution Bus (to Load Switch Controllers).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A high-efficiency, fixed-frequency synchronous boost converter IC. It uses internal MOSFETs and an external inductor/capacitor to generate a regulated 5V output, capable of supplying the required high current for peripherals with good transient response.

Functional Modules.Module Name: Load Switch Controllers for Hot-Plug Interfaces
Functional Modules.Function Description: Individually controls power delivery to high-current peripheral connectors (e.g., HDMI, Micro SD) with inrush current limiting and fault protection.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: 5V Rail from Boost Converter, Individual enable signals from system controller (e.g., GPIOs), Fault feedback (optional).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: HDMI connector power pin, Micro SD card VDD pin, System controller (fault status).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Dedicated load switch ICs per interface. Each integrates a low-Rds(on) MOSFET gate driver with adjustable slew rate (soft-start) control to limit inrush current during hot-plug. Includes over-current protection and thermal shutdown. Enabled only when the peripheral is activated.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Manages the enable/disable sequencing of power rails, monitors system status, and controls the load switches based on operational state.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: User input, peripheral detection signals (e.g., card detect), signals from charger module, voltage monitor outputs.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Enable pins of Boost Converter and Load Switch Controllers, Status LEDs, Charger IC control pins.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A low-power microcontroller or dedicated power sequencer IC. It follows a predefined sequence: ensures 3.3V is stable, enables the 5V boost converter, then enables specific load switches based on system mode. It also handles fault recovery and provides status communication.
